articles.title: Claude Code Can Now Build and Test iOS Apps in Apple's Simulator - MacRumors
articles.description: [www.macrumors.com] Tuesday July 7, 2026 1:05 pm PDT by Juli Clover Anthropic today said it is bringing Claude Cowork to mobile and the web, with beta access rolling out to Max users first. Claude Cowork is a feature that lets Claude access local files and connected tools, then autonomously complete tasks on your behalf. Claude Cowork tasks can now be started and monitored on the web, desktop, and Claude mobile apps. Claude is able to work in the background... Read Full Article • 20 comments Top Rated Comments S Sp1281 3 hours ago at 04:50 pm I guess Im a developer now. Score: 14 Votes (Like | Disagree) CalMin 2 [9to5mac.com] > 3. Watch the app in the simulator pane: When the app launches in a simulator, the iOS Simulator pane opens next to the conversation. The first time Claude uses a device, the desktop app asks you to allow it; see Grant Claude access to a device. Claude installs the app, taps through it, and reads the screen to verify its own changes while you watch. Anthropic explains that “the simulator pane drives the simulator directly, so it doesn’t need computer use and never takes over your screen or hides your other windows.” From the CLI, however, “Claude reaches the iOS Simulator through computer use

articles.title: AMD commits up to $5 billion to Anthropic
articles.description: AMD says it's going to invest up to $5 billion in Anthropic, while helping to expand the AI company's computing power, according to an announcement on Wednesday. As part of the new partnership, Anthropic will deploy up to 2 gigawatts of AMD's Instinct MI450 AI GPUs using the chipmaker's new Helios rack-scale system, as reported […]

articles.title: Google launches a cheaper alternative to large AI security models like Mythos
articles.description: Google is launching Gemini 3.6 Flash alongside a new security model dedicated to quickly finding and patching security vulnerabilities. In a blog post on Tuesday, Google describes Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber as a "cost-efficient and highly capable alternative" to larger, more expensive AI systems, such as the one offered by Anthropic's Mythos. The cybersecurity model […]

articles.title: Meta made its own AI detection system. It should have just used Google’s
articles.description: IIn March, Meta's Oversight Board called on the company to "meet its public commitments and employ its own tools" to help quell the spread of deceptive generative AI content across platforms. Meta responded in July by introducing Content Seal - an invisible watermarking technology that flags images generated by the company's new AI model. But […]

articles.title: Utility companies promise to spare us from AI’s energy bill
articles.description: In the face of backlash to concerns the AI boom will increase consumer electricity bills, the largest utility companies and data center developers in the US are now promising to do something about it. The Wall Street Journal reports that nearly 200 organizations have signed President Donald Trump's "rate payer protection pledge" that's meant to […]

articles.title: Here’s what Samsung’s smart glasses actually look like
articles.description: Samsung has given us our first chance to check out its upcoming smart glasses in person, revealing two new designs and the first specs in the process, including an impressive 9-hour battery life. The glasses, developed in collaboration with Google and the eyewear brands Gentle Monster and Warby Parker, are due to launch this fall. […]

articles.title: Halliday’s latest smart glasses feature a much-improved display
articles.description: I first slipped on Halliday's original smart glasses at CES 2025. I was not a fan. The glasses had a tiny, movable display window embedded into the frame that was incredibly finicky to see, and my 30-minute demo left me with achy eyeballs. It was an interesting concept with terrible execution, especially compared to the […]

items.title: Claude Code Can Now Build and Test iOS Apps in Apple's Simulator - MacRumors
items.summary: Anthropic将Claude Cowork扩展至移动端和网页版，Claude Code支持在Mac的iOS模拟器中构建测试应用。
items.why_it_matters: 降低iOS开发门槛，开发者可在桌面端直接测试应用。

items.title: AMD commits up to $5 billion to Anthropic
items.summary: AMD承诺向Anthropic投资最高50亿美元，后者将部署最高2吉瓦的AMD Instinct MI450 AI GPU。
items.why_it_matters: 增强Anthropic算力基础，挑战英伟达在AI芯片领域的主导地位。

items.title: Google launches a cheaper alternative to large AI security models like Mythos
items.summary: Google推出Gemini 3.6 Flash及安全模型Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber，定位为高价AI安全系统的低成本替代方案。
items.why_it_matters: 降低企业使用AI进行安全漏洞检测的成本。

items.title: Meta made its own AI detection system. It should have just used Google’s
items.summary: Meta七月推出Content Seal隐形水印技术，用于标记其新AI模型生成的图像。
items.why_it_matters: 回应平台治理要求，但自研水印效果可能不及现有行业方案。

items.title: Utility companies promise to spare us from AI’s energy bill
items.summary: 美国近200家电力公司与数据中心开发商签署费率纳税人保护承诺，称将避免AI发展推高消费者电费。
items.why_it_matters: 缓解公众对AI能耗导致电费上涨的担忧，为数据中心扩张争取政策支持。
